title: Influences on the curriculum
summary: 9699 — who controls the curriculum, hidden curriculum, and ideological influences on knowledge.
learningObjectives: Analyse government, employers, and interest groups' influence.
Evaluate Marxist and New Right views of curriculum content.
Assess cultural bias in subject selection and textbooks.

simpleExplanation.title: What gets taught — and why
simpleExplanation.summary: The official curriculum reflects power struggles over which knowledge counts; the hidden curriculum teaches conformity and hierarchy.
steps: Gendered and ethnocentric bias in content.

flashcards.back: Official planned subjects, timetables, exam specifications.
Implicit messages — authority, competition, conformity (Bowles & Gintis).
Dominant groups define 'legitimate knowledge' to maintain power.
Content centred on white British/European history and culture.
Subjects stereotyped — STEM male, care subjects female.

sections.content: **Curriculum influences** (5.3) completes Topic 5. Contrast **Apple**, **Young**, and **Ball** on knowledge control. Link hidden curriculum to **5.1 Marxism**.

sections.question: Assess the view that the hidden curriculum is the most important thing schools teach. [15 marks]
sections.solution:
**Hidden curriculum defined:** Unofficial norms — **obedience**, **punctuality**, **competition**, acceptance of **hierarchy**.

**Marxist case:** Bowles & Gintis — prepares **docile workforce**; more important than subject content for capitalism; **social control** over working-class pupils.

**Counter:** **Formal curriculum** teaches **literacy, skills**, exam technique needed for jobs; **pupil agency** — not all accept messages (Willis); **national curriculum** reforms aim at **standards** not just control.

**Evaluation:** Hidden curriculum **real and significant** especially for **socialisation** — but **overstated** if ignoring **cognitive gains** and **diverse school cultures**.

**Conclusion:** **Important complement** to formal curriculum, not always **most important** for every pupil.
